items.title: Lilly, Astellas circle Sangamo assets as biotech files for bankruptcy
items.snippet: As Sangamo runs out of cash, Eli Lilly and Astellas have emerged as stalking horse bidders for key assets, including a Fabry gene therapy currently being submitted for potential FDA approval.

items.title: Layoff Tracker: Novartis lets go of 60 Tourmaline workers after takeover
items.snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

items.title: Merck posts ‘encouraging’ Phase 3 ulcerative colitis data but Roche challenge looms
items.snippet: Given its intravenous route of administration, Merck’s tulisokibart will likely “need to be meaningfully improved” over Roche’s afimkibart, which can be given subcutaneously, BMO Capital Markets analysts said. Both assets are being tested for ulcerative colitis.

items.title: Lilly’s tirzepatide will be biggest blockbuster ever, topping $70B by 2032: Evaluate
items.snippet: Eli Lilly’s weight-loss franchise—including the tirzepatide products Mounjaro and Zepbound, and the weight-loss pill Foundayo—is projected to account for nearly half of the total sales of the top 10 drugs in 2032.

items.title: Can ADCs be kinder and gentler? Join BioSpace at BIO to find out
items.snippet: While existing antibody-drug conjugates have been incredible advancements in cancer care, patients are ready for a better experience. At the BIO International Convention in San Diego on Thursday, BioSpace will lead a discussion on the next generation of ADCs.

items.title: Pfizer delivers ‘disappointing’ late-stage lung cancer data for ADC from $43B Seagen buy
items.snippet: Despite an overall survival miss, Leerink Partners said Pfizer’s antibody-drug conjugate showed “promising” signals of efficacy in a subgroup of patients who had undergone only one prior line of treatment.

items.title: BIO 2026: Biopharma dealmakers seek to protect ‘premium pricing’ from Trump’s MFN policy
items.snippet: With drug pricing now embedded in U.S. policy, business development teams in biotech and pharma are changing the way they strike deals, including acknowledging policy uncertainties with renegotiation clauses.

items.title: BIO 2026: US public health as we know it is gone. Can we rebuild a better system?
items.snippet: Former FDA, CDC and NIH leaders convene at the BIO International Convention to discuss the dismantling of the Department of Health and Human Services under the Trump administration—and where we go from here.

items.title: With onshoring ‘having a moment,’ Aquestive CEO shares lessons from 20 years of US manufacturing
items.snippet: Having stuck with local production in the years when offshoring to lower-cost locations was in fashion, Aquestive’s CEO is well placed to explain what the recent reshoring drive means for manufacturers.

items.title: How US pharma manufacturing pay stacks up to other jobs in the industry
items.snippet: Anticipated to be one of fastest-growing jobs in manufacturing, pharmaceutical jobs offer a salary 32% higher than the average U.S. manufacturing role.

items.title: Nuvectis, Haisco ink up to $1.4B deal for two late-stage programs
items.snippet: China’s Haisco Pharmaceutical continues to wield deals, this time out-licensing rights to two late-stage programs to New Jersey biotech Nuvectis.

items.title: HHS debuts ‘unprecedented’ initiative to ‘restore American leadership’ in clinical trials
items.snippet: Among the health department’s efforts is an expedited investigational new drug pilot program that would leverage collaborations with U.S. research institutions to reduce early trial timelines by as much as 12 months.

items.title: REGENXBIO to resubmit application for Hunter gene therapy after FDA’s most recent pivot
items.snippet: The resubmission for RGX-121, expected in the third quarter, comes as the FDA has deemed REGENXBIO’s existing data “sufficient” to support an accelerated filing. It immediately follows a similar reversal of position regarding uniQure’s embattled Huntington’s disease gene therapy.

items.title: Definium’s LSD-based therapy elicits ‘profound’ efficacy in Phase 3 depression trial
items.snippet: Results for Definium Therapeutics psychedelic candidate for major depressive disorder “exceeded expectations,” according to Stifel, while Jefferies called the efficacy data “profound.”

items.title: Sofinnova, Sanofi alum unveil Bionyra with $165M and next-gen immunology assets
items.snippet: Formed by Sofinnova Partners and former Sanofi executive Frédéric Marrache, Bionyra Pharma emerged from stealth Monday with an initial focus in inflammatory bowel disease and atopic dermatitis.

items.title: Sanofi’s R&D head exits, with AI biotech Xaira’s exec stepping in
items.snippet: About two months into CEO Belén Garijo’s new tenure, Sanofi’s R&D chief has departed, with Xaira’s chief medical officer set to take charge of the pharma’s pipeline.

items.title: UniQure’s chronic epilepsy gene therapy 'should be on investors’ radar'
items.snippet: As uniQure prepares its closely watched Huntington’s disease gene therapy for FDA review, the biotech is also making waves in chronic epilepsy with another gene therapy that Stifel says is off to a "promising start."

items.title: Lilly bets up to $800M for BioArctic’s brain delivery tech
items.snippet: Eli Lilly plans to use BioArctic’s technology to shuttle an undisclosed drug candidate into the brain. The pharma hasn’t specified which neurodegenerative disease it will target.

items.title: Insilico’s AI design engine bags deal with Korean biotech worth possible $2.5B+
items.snippet: Insilico Medicine and SK Biopharmaceuticals will use generative AI to develop novel therapies for unspecified neuroimmune disorders.

items.title: AbbVie strikes $10.9B Apogee buy to challenge juggernaut Dupixent
items.snippet: AbbVie will add Apogee’s IL-23 blocker to its current immunology stalwarts Skyrizi and Rinvoq, which have helped the pharma ride out the steep patent cliff left behind from mega-blockbuster drug Humira.

items.title: BIO 2026: Cell and gene therapy sector must break the mold to thrive
items.snippet: If cell and gene therapy makers are going to achieve their mission to improve patients’ lives, the industry must come together to share information across stakeholders, from regulators to manufacturers to payers.

items.title: At FDA, ‘caretaker mode’ is better than power trip mode
items.snippet: UniQure’s planned third-quarter submission for its Huntington’s disease gene therapy may be a harbinger of a more flexible FDA under acting commissioner Kyle Diamantas—but how long will it last? And how can companies be sure these positive decisions won’t just be reversed?

items.title: Lilly just bought renowned regulator Peter Marks a pipeline to play with
items.snippet: FDA veteran Peter Marks will now shape the future of Eli Lilly’s vaccines work after the buys of Curevo, LimmaTech Biologics and Vaccine Company for up to $3.8 billion total.

items.title: 3 top FDA, CDC roles filled by 15 different people in less than 18 months
items.snippet: The Department of Health and Human Services is spinning its wheels, unable to establish steady leadership at three major divisions—the CDC and the FDA’s two primary review units.
